title: HSBC Bank Australia Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered HSBC Bank Australia Limited to pay a $35 million penalty — one of the first cases of its kind globally — after the bank admitted serious failures protecting customers from scams. Between January 2020 and August 2024, HSBC received more than 1,000 reports of unauthorised transactions totalling $34.6 million, and admitted inadequate controls on its internal transfer system between May 2023 and May 2024, a 380% surge in unauthorised-transaction reports in FY24, and average investigation times of 144 days. Approximately $21.5 million in compensation had been paid to affected customers by the time of judgment, with further payments expected, plus an additional $6.5 million in recovered funds returned.
scope: Federal Court civil penalty of $35 million, admitted, for scam-protection failures — one of the first cases of its kind globally; Admitted inadequate internal transfer system controls and average scam-investigation times of 144 days; More than 1,000 unauthorised-transaction reports totalling $34.6 million between January 2020 and August 2024
